courses.title: Pediatric & Child Health Nursing
outcomes: Apply growth and development theories to pediatric nursing care across age groups.
Demonstrate assessment skills specific to infants, toddlers, children, and adolescents.
Formulate nursing care plans for pediatric infectious, respiratory, cardiovascular, and gastrointestinal conditions.
Apply safety, dosage calculation, and family-centered care principles in pediatric drug administration.
Identify signs of pediatric emergencies, dehydration, and acute distress for immediate escalation.

courses.title: Community Health Nursing II
outcomes: Assess community health needs using epidemiological and demographic data.
Design and implement community-based health promotion and disease prevention programs.
Evaluate environmental health hazards, sanitation, and vector control in community settings in Pakistan.
Demonstrate home visit protocols, family assessment, and primary health care delivery.
Collaborate with local public health agencies, immunization programs (EPI), and maternal-child health clinics.

courses.title: Nursing Research I
outcomes: Explain the importance of evidence-based practice and research in professional nursing.
Formulate clinical research questions using the PICO framework.
Critique quantitative and qualitative nursing research literature.
Explain research design, sampling methods, data collection tools, and ethical considerations.
Develop a formal nursing research proposal.

courses.title: Nursing Leadership and Management
outcomes: Apply leadership theories, management principles, and decision-making models in clinical environments.
Demonstrate effective delegation, conflict resolution, time management, and team communication.
Explain healthcare quality improvement, patient safety protocols, and risk management.
Differentiate organizational structures, resource allocation, and staffing models in hospital units.
